zh.Radbourne, Derbyshire, England: 英國德比夏爾拉德布恩
zh.Henage, Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Englan: 英國英格蘭德比夏爾斯特費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Peter de la Pole於大約1377年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾時，他的父親Sir John de la Pole II35歲，母親Cecilia de Wakebridge34歲。他於大約1410年與Elizabeth de Laughton在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1432年8月4日在英國德比夏爾拉德布恩享年56歲。

zh-hans.Radbourne, Derbyshire, England: 英国德比夏爾拉德布恩
zh-hans.Henage, Chesterfield, Derbyshire, Englan: 英国英格兰德比夏爾斯特費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Peter de la Pole于大约1377年出生在英国英格兰德比夏爾时，他的父亲Sir John de la Pole II35岁，母亲Cecilia de Wakebridge34岁。他于大约1410年与Elizabeth de Laughton在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1432年8月4日在英国德比夏爾拉德布恩享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Peter de la Pole was born about 1377, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John de la Pole II, was 35 and his mother, Cecilia de Wakebridge, was 34. He married Elizabeth de Laughton about 1410, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 4 August 1432, in Radbourne, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Peter de la Pole est né environ 1377, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John de la Pole II, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Cecilia de Wakebridge, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Laughton environ 1410, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 4 août 1432, à Radbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Peter de la Pole um 1377 in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de la Pole II 35 und seine Mutter Cecilia de Wakebridge 34. Er hat um 1410 in England Elizabeth de Laughton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 4. August 1432 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Radbourne, Derbyshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Peter de la Pole è nato il circa 1377 nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John de la Pole II, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Cecilia de Wakebridge, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth de Laughton circa 1410, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 4 agosto 1432, nel Radbourne, Derbyshire, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Peter de la Poleが約1377年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John de la Pole IIは35歳，母Cecilia de Wakebridgeは34歳であった。約1410年にイギリスでElizabeth de Laughtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1432年8月4日にイギリスデルブイシュアイアラドボウルンで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Peter de la Pole(이)가 대략 1377년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de la Pole II(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecilia de Wakebridge(은)는 34세였다. 그는 대략 1410년 영국에서 Elizabeth de Laughton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1432년 8월 4일 영국 더비실르 라드보우른에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Peter de la Pole nasceu aproximadamente 1377, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John de la Pole II, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia de Wakebridge, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Laughton aproximadamente 1410, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1432, em Radbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Peter de la Pole родился около 1377 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John de la Pole II было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecilia de Wakebridge, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Laughton около 1410 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 4 августа 1432 г. в местности радбурн, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Peter de la Pole nació aproximadamente 1377, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John de la Pole II, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Cecilia de Wakebridge, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Elizabeth de Laughton aproximadamente 1410, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 4 de agosto de 1432, en Radbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años.
